description:
A data-science colleague left you a small, dirty dataset and asked for two things:

    1) Clean the data.
    2) Capture a very short snapshot of system-resource usage that runs **in parallel** with the cleaning job.

    The colleague wants everything stored under the following fixed locations (already created for you):

        /home/user/data/
        /home/user/monitoring/

    Existing, dirty CSV:
        /home/user/data/raw_sales.csv

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    GOAL
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    When you are finished there must be **three new files** and their
    exact characteristics must match what is written below.

    1.  /home/user/data/clean_sales.csv
        • Keep the header row from raw_sales.csv unchanged.
        • Remove every data row that has **any** empty field.
          (That is, if a column value is blank, drop that entire record.)
        • Use the same comma separator and line-ending style as the input.

    2.  /home/user/monitoring/sys_usage.log
        • While your cleaning command is running, simultaneously run
          “vmstat 1 5” (i.e. 5 samples, 1-second spacing) and redirect its
          full, unmodified stdout to this file.
        • Because vmstat prints two header lines followed by 5 data lines,
          the log must end up containing exactly seven (7) non-blank lines.
          Do not post-process or truncate the output.

    3.  /home/user/monitoring/cleaning_summary.log
        • After both of the above activities complete, append the following
          two lines (and nothing else, no extra spaces or blank lines).
          Replace the angle-bracketed placeholders with real integers:

              CleanedRows: <number_of_rows_in_clean_sales_csv_excluding_header>
              MonitoringLines: <number_of_nonblank_lines_in_sys_usage.log>

          Example of the *exact* required format:

              CleanedRows: 3
              MonitoringLines: 7

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    CONSTRAINTS
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    • The monitoring (vmstat) and the data-cleaning must execute in parallel
      (e.g., launch vmstat in the background and run the cleaning in the
      foreground, or vice-versa, before waiting for both to finish).
    • Do not run anything as root; regular user privileges are enough.
    • No external datasets or internet access are permitted—work only with
      the files already under /home/user.
    • The automated grader will verify:
        – clean_sales.csv exists and has the correct header plus only the
          fully-populated records.
        – sys_usage.log exists and has exactly 7 non-blank lines.
        – cleaning_summary.log exists and its two lines match the precise
          format and correct values.
    • If anything at all is different (wrong paths, extra spaces, wrong
      newline spacing, etc.) the task will be considered failed, so double-check
      your output files.

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    HINTS (optional)
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    • A quick way to delete rows with empty fields is with awk, csvkit,
      or a tiny Python/Perl script—choose whatever feels easiest.
    • To ensure the commands overlap in time, start one with “&” and then
      promptly launch the other before calling “wait”.
    • Counting lines without the header can be done with “tail -n +2”.
truth:
# ---------- Pre-existing filesystem state ----------
    # Directory structure:
    #   /home/user/data/           (700, writable by user)
    #   /home/user/monitoring/     (700, writable by user)

    # File already present before the agent starts:
    /home/user/data/raw_sales.csv
    ----------------------------------------------------
    id,product,price,quantity
    1,Widget,19.99,5
    2,Gadget,,3
    3,Thingamabob,4.99,
    4,Widget,19.99,2
    5,Gizmo,8.49,0
    ----------------------------------------------------

    # ---------- Expected files after successful completion ----------

    /home/user/data/clean_sales.csv
    ----------------------------------------------------
    id,product,price,quantity
    1,Widget,19.99,5
    4,Widget,19.99,2
    5,Gizmo,8.49,0
    ----------------------------------------------------

    /home/user/monitoring/sys_usage.log
    # Must consist of exactly 7 non-blank lines (actual numeric
    # values will vary, so they are not fixed here).
    # The automated test will validate:
    #     wc -l /home/user/monitoring/sys_usage.log == 7
    #     No blank lines present.

    /home/user/monitoring/cleaning_summary.log
    ----------------------------------------------------
    CleanedRows: 3
    MonitoringLines: 7
    ----------------------------------------------------
